title: Christmas Day
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
December 25, 2019 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is Christmas Day proper, the Feast of the Nativity of Our Lord Jesus Christ, observed on the actual date rather than the previous Sunday. The readings follow the RCL/BCP Christmas Day Eucharist: Isaiah 52:7-10, Psalm 98, Hebrews 1:1-12, and John 1:1-14. The church year is in the Christmas season, which began on December 24 and extends through Epiphany (January 6, 2020), following the conclusion of Advent four days earlier on December 21.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of the 2018 General Convention held in Austin, Texas, with no major General Convention scheduled until 2021. The Diocese of Colorado was led by Bishop Robert James O'Neill, who had served since 2009 and would continue through this period without major diocesan controversy dominating news. No significant Episcopal-specific news events marked the immediate week before Christmas 2019.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical winter weather conditions in late December with temperatures in the mid-30s Fahrenheit and clear skies through the holiday. The city was focused on holiday shopping and celebrations rather than major breaking news; the Colorado legislature was in recess. Saint John's Cathedral sits on 17th Avenue near the downtown Capitol Hill area, which would have hosted typical Christmas Eve and Christmas Day celebrations with reduced traffic and normal holiday closures.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump impeachment dominated headlines, with the House of Representatives having voted to impeach President Trump on December 18, 2019 on charges of abuse of power and obstruction of Congress—just one week before this sermon. The Senate trial was not yet underway but would begin on January 16, 2020. Christmas 2019 was economically robust, with strong holiday retail sales, and the unemployment rate remained low at 3.5%. The COVID-19 pandemic had not yet affected US consciousness—the first US case would not be confirmed until January 21, 2020.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Syrian civil war continued with Russia and Turkey consolidating control over northwest Syria; Bashar al-Assad's position had strengthened considerably by late 2019. The Hong Kong pro-democracy protests, which had dominated headlines throughout 2019, were still ongoing but had somewhat diminished in frequency by late December after months of intense unrest. The UK had recently completed Brexit negotiations under Boris Johnson's leadership, with the Trade and Cooperation Agreement finalized on December 24, 2019, literally the day before this sermon.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The cultural moment was one of deep political polarization in America amid the impeachment crisis, yet Christmas itself provided a temporary overlay of traditional holiday observance and consumerism. The zeitgeist carried anxiety about political division, though the economic outlook remained optimistic and unemployment was low. Popular culture was dominated by streaming content (Netflix, Disney+), and the release of Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker on December 20 was the major entertainment event of the week—a franchise milestone that would be widely discussed in Colorado social circles.
